title: Bandwidth signaling for a basic service set (bss) supporting 320 mhz operating bandwidth
abstract: This disclosure provides systems, devices, apparatus and methods, including computer programs encoded on storage media, for techniques for an access point (AP) in a basic service set (BSS) to advertise or indicate 320 MHz bandwidth operation and frequency segment composition in the 320 MHz bandwidth to a station (STA) in a Wireless Local Area Network (WLAN). Also, a WLAN device may advertise or indicate its 320 MHz bandwidth support capability to other WLAN devices in the WLAN.
claims:
Claims 1. A method performed by an access point (AP), comprising: determining to advertise a 320 MHz operating bandwidth for a basic service set (BSS) associated with the AP; selecting a frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth from a plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth; and preparing a management frame for transmission to at least one station (STA) of a wireless local area network (WLAN), the management frame indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth associated with the BSS and the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining a center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, wherein the management frame further indicating the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: transmitting the management frame to the at least one STA of the BSS, the management frame indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the management frame includes an extremely high throughput (EHT) Operation Information Element (IE), the EHT Operation IE indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and the center frequency of each segment 

 associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The method of claim 4, wherein an Operation Bandwidth field of the EHT Operation IE further includes a primary channel number of a primary channel of the BSS, and a channel width for providing a composition of the 320MHz operating bandwidth and a number of frequency segments. The method of claim 4, wherein an Operation Bandwidth field of the EHT Operation IE further includes a first segment channel center frequency, a second segment channel center frequency, a third segment channel center frequency, and a fourth segment channel center frequency for indicating the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving bandwidth capabilities information from STAs of the BSS, the bandwidth capabilities information indicating an operating bandwidth and one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by each of the STAs of the BSS; and determining to advertise the 320 MHz operating bandwidth for the BSS in response to determining at least one of the STAs supports the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving bandwidth capabilities information from STAs of the BSS, the bandwidth capabilities information indicating an operating bandwidth and one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by each of the STAs of the BSS; and selecting the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth from the plurality of frequency segment compositions associated 

 with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth based, at least in part, on the one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by each of the STAs of the BSS. 9. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining one or more STAs of the BSS are EHT devices and one or more STAs of the BSS are non-EHT devices; and preparing the management frame for transmission, the management frame including an EHT Operation IE indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, the frequency segment composition, and a center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the EHT devices, and a non-EHT Operation IE indicating the operating bandwidth and a corresponding frequency segment composition for the non- EHT devices. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the management frame is a Beacon Frame, a Probe Response Frame, or an Association Response Frame. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the frequency segment composition selected for the BSS is one of the plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, wherein the plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth include a single 320 MHz frequency segment, two 160 MHz frequency segments, four 80 MHz frequency segments, and one 160 MHz frequency segment and two 80 MHz frequency segments. 12. A method performed by a wireless local area network (WLAN) device, comprising: determining support for a 320 MHz operating bandwidth by the WLAN device; determining one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by the WLAN device from a plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth; and 

 preparing a management frame for transmission to at least one other WLAN device of a WLAN, the management frame indicating support for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth and support for the one or more frequency segment compositions for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the management frame includes an extremely high throughput (EHT) Capabilities Information Element (IE), the EHT Capabilities IE including an indication of whether the WLAN device supports the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and an indication of the one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by the WLAN device. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the WLAN device is a station (STA), further comprising: receiving operating bandwidth information from an access point (AP) associated with a basic service set (BSS), the operating bandwidth information indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth for the BSS associated with the AP, a frequency segment composition for the BSS, and a center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition associated with the BSS; joining the BSS associated with the AP; and preparing a packet for transmission in a channel of the BSS using the 320 MHz operating bandwidth and the frequency segment composition, the packet including an indication of the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the operating bandwidth information includes an Operation Bandwidth field of an EHT Operation IE, the Operation Bandwidth field of the EHT Operation IE including a primary channel number of a primary channel of the BSS, a channel width for providing a composition of the 320MHz operating bandwidth, and a plurality of segment channel center frequencies for indicating the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition associated with the BSS. 16. The method of claim 12, wherein the WLAN device is an AP, further comprising: determining one or more STAs of a BSS associated with the AP are EHT devices and one or more STAs of the BSS are non-EHT devices; and preparing the management frame for transmission, the management frame including an EHT Capabilities IE indicating support for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth and support for the one or more frequency segment compositions for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and a non-EHT Capabilities IE indicating bandwidth capabilities for the non-EHT devices. 17. The method of claim 12, wherein the WLAN device is a STA, wherein the management frame is a Probe Request Frame or an Association Request Frame. 18. The method of claim 12, wherein the WLAN device is an AP, wherein the management frame is a Beacon Frame, a Probe Response Frame, or an Association Response Frame. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein the one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by the WLAN device include at least one member selected from the group consisting of a single 320 MHz frequency segment, two 160 MHz frequency segments, four 80 MHz frequency segments, and one 160 MHz frequency segment and two 80 MHz frequency segments. 20. An access point (AP), comprising: a processor; and memory having instructions stored therein which, when executed by the processor, cause the AP to: determine to advertise a 320 MHz operating bandwidth for a basic service set (BSS) associated with the AP; select a frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth from a plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth; and 

 prepare a management frame for transmission to at least one station (STA) of a wireless local area network (WLAN), the management frame indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth associated with the BSS and the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The AP of claim 20, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the AP to: determine a center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, wherein the management frame further indicating the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The AP of claim 21, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the AP to: transmit the management frame to the at least one STA of the BSS, the management frame indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The AP of claim 21, wherein the management frame includes an extremely high throughput (EHT) Operation Information Element (IE), the EHT Operation IE indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The AP of claim 23, wherein an Operation Bandwidth field of the EHT Operation IE further includes: a primary channel number of a primary channel of the BSS, 

 a channel width for providing a composition of the 320MHz operating bandwidth and a number of frequency segments; and a first segment channel center frequency, a second segment channel center frequency, a third segment channel center frequency, and a fourth segment channel center frequency for indicating the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The AP of claim 20, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the AP to: receive bandwidth capabilities information from STAs of the BSS, the bandwidth capabilities information indicating an operating bandwidth and one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by each of the STAs of the BSS; and determine to advertise the 320 MHz operating bandwidth for the BSS in response to a determination that at least one of the STAs supports the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The AP of claim 20, wherein the frequency segment composition selected for the BSS is one of the plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, wherein the plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth include a single 320 MHz frequency segment, two 160 MHz frequency segments, four 80 MHz frequency segments, and one 160 MHz frequency segment and two 80 MHz frequency segments. A computer-readable storage medium having instructions stored therein, which when executed by a processor of an access point (AP), cause the AP to: determine to advertise a 320 MHz operating bandwidth for a basic service set (BSS) associated with the AP; 

 select a frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth from a plurality of frequency segment compositions associated with the 320 MHz operating bandwidth; and prepare a management frame for transmission to at least one station (STA) of a wireless local area network (WLAN), the management frame indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth associated with the BSS and the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 27, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the AP to: determine a center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, wherein the management frame further indicating the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 28, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the AP to: transmit the management frame to the at least one STA of the BSS, the management frame indicating the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth, and the center frequency of each segment associated with the frequency segment composition for the 320 MHz operating bandwidth. The computer-readable storage medium of claim 27, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the AP to: receive bandwidth capabilities information from STAs of the BSS, the bandwidth capabilities information indicating an operating bandwidth and one or more frequency segment compositions that are supported by each of the STAs of the BSS; and 

 determine to advertise the 320 MHz operating bandwidth for the BSS in response to determining at least one of the STAs supports the 320 MHz operating bandwidth.
